rationale: In the theory section, De Angelis et al. is not just listed; its core finding is summarized and used to motivate the broader question of motives in retelling. The authors explicitly contrast prior WOM work (including this citation) with their own focus on retelling form and sequential distortion, making it part of the paper's conceptual positioning and gap statement. The citation is therefore more than a passing background mention, but it serves a framing role rather than direct method adoption or explicit critique.
relation_description: The citing paper uses De Angelis et al. (2012) to establish that WOM motives, especially self-enhancement, can shape the valence of what people share (positive about self, negative about others). It then positions its own contribution as moving from that prior focus on what gets shared to how retellings are rewritten across waves, emphasizing detail loss, opinion injection, and negativity.
